mem.stage_1_foundation: In daily life, do you often forget a word you just memorized? Or find yourself always looking for your keys or forgetting your homework before school? These are signs that your memory isn't being effectively integrated.
mem.stage_2_transition: In daily life, do you often find yourself walking into a room and forgetting what you went there for? Or forgetting a new client's name right after meeting them? Sometimes words are on the tip of your tongue but you can't recall them—these are warning signs of memory slip.
mem.stage_3_senior: In daily life, do you often forget where you put things? Or occasionally forget if you've taken your medicine, and repeat the same questions? These are clear signals that your brain's memory areas need retraining.

rea.stage_1_foundation: You might find yourself always half a beat slower than others when playing games or sports; or taking a long time to respond when a teacher asks a question. Your brain's reaction nerves need to be reawakened.
rea.stage_2_transition: You might notice needing to pause before responding in conversations, or that your body reacts half a beat too slow to sudden traffic conditions while driving or riding. This increases hidden risks in daily life.
rea.stage_3_senior: You might notice your movements becoming more sluggish, or failing to react in time to sudden events like an item falling. Decreased reaction time affects mobility safety; neural transmission needs restimulation.

spa.stage_1_foundation: Do you often feel stuck when doing puzzles or assembling models? Or easily get lost and confused about directions in unfamiliar places? Weakened spatial perception affects your grasp of 3D environments.
spa.stage_2_transition: Do you highly depend on GPS when going out, or even make wrong turns while looking at it? Or fail to judge distances when parking in crowded places? This means your spatial perception needs strengthening.
spa.stage_3_senior: When you go out, has your judgment of direction become blurrier? Or do you occasionally misjudge the distance and knock things over when reaching for a cup? Loss of spatial awareness can be recovered through specific training.

att.stage_1_foundation: Do you recently find it hard to settle down and study? Your thoughts wander quickly, and you're easily distracted by classmates or phone notifications? Inability to concentrate greatly reduces your learning efficiency.
att.stage_2_transition: Do you recently find it hard to focus on a document or report? Your train of thought is easily interrupted, and you even zone out during meetings? Insufficient focus drastically lowers work efficiency; your brain needs to relearn how to 'focus'.
att.stage_3_senior: Do you recently find it hard to focus through a whole TV program? Or struggle to concentrate and finish listening when someone is speaking? Decreased focus affects your ability to receive new information; the brain needs to relearn to focus.
